medications.indication_rationale: Continue the prescribed thyroxine unchanged; do not alter the dose without current thyroid function tests and medical guidance.
Increasing thyroxine without bloods risks iatrogenic thyrotoxicosis/arrhythmia and does not address a possible decompensation or adrenal issue.

medications.drug_name: oral rehydration and small easily-digestible meals
medications.indication_rationale: Restore hydration and calories in a patient who hasn't eaten/drunk for ~24h; simple, safe supportive care while assessment is arranged.

allied_health_referrals.rationale: Evidence-based dietary support for wellbeing — not as an autoimmune 'cure'.
Living alone and unable to self-care during flares may warrant practical support.

integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Some evidence for a modest reduction in TPO antibody titres; does not reliably change symptoms or replace thyroxine.

integrative_alternative_therapies.therapy_name: Strict gluten/dairy elimination (Wahls-style diet) as primary therapy
integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: No robust evidence that gluten/dairy elimination modifies autoimmune thyroiditis; should not be presented as disease-modifying or as a substitute for assessment.
Insufficient evidence in autoimmune thyroiditis; low harm at usual doses but not established therapy.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Watch for and report becoming very drowsy/hard to wake, breathless, colder, or vomiting/fainting, and keep warm.
Have small, warm, protein-containing meals and regular fluids; a warm meal within ~30 minutes of waking helps stabilise energy.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Restores intake and energy without unproven restrictive diets.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Keep taking thyroxine exactly as prescribed and do not change the dose or start supplements without medical advice.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Prevents iatrogenic harm and maintains replacement.

patient_education_points.point: Your thyroid gland is underactive due to an autoimmune condition; that explains the exhaustion, feeling cold and foggy thinking. A normal blood test in the past doesn't guarantee your levels are right now, so we need to recheck them.

Please don't change your thyroxine dose or start supplements/diets on your own — some of the 'anti-inflammatory cure' claims aren't supported by evidence and changing your dose without a blood test can be harmful.
Because you're on your own, very weak and haven't eaten or drunk much, you need to be seen in person soon with blood tests to make sure this isn't something more serious than a flare.
Call 000 if you become too drowsy to stay awake to eat or drink, feel too weak to breathe normally, get much colder, or start vomiting or fainting.

follow_up_plan.interval: Prompt in-person GP review within 24–48 hours with urgent bloods (sooner if any red flag)
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Consciousness/vitals, TFTs and TPO, electrolytes/glucose/cortisol, hydration/nutrition, thyroxine adequacy
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Unrousable drowsiness, breathlessness, worsening cold, vomiting or fainting — call 000.

must_include_items: Screen and safety-net myxedema coma and adrenal crisis (consciousness, breathing, temperature, vomiting/fainting)
Advise continuing thyroxine unchanged and NOT altering dose or relying on supplements/diet
Arrange prompt in-person review with urgent bloods (TFTs, electrolytes, glucose, cortisol)
acceptable_alternatives: Same-day in-person assessment if the patient is very weak/unable to self-care
Selenium mentioned neutrally as low-evidence, not as a cure
errors_of_commission: Promoting Wahls/gluten-dairy elimination or LDN as disease-modifying therapy
Reassuring away a possible endocrine emergency, or sending to ED by ambulance despite a reassuring screen
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires (1) myxedema + adrenal-crisis safety-netting, (2) continue-thyroxine / no-unguided-dose-change advice, and (3) prompt in-person review with bloods. Endorsing dose changes or unproven diets/supplements as therapy is a fail.
